name: pyspat

metadata.name: pySpat
metadata.summary: Python based spatial statistics package. Inspired by, and completely unaffiliated with, R's spatstat.

documentation: Work in progress project for spatial statistical methods under one python based roof.

functions.name: pySpat.utils.random_window
documentation:
Generate a random rectangular observation window.

Parameters:
----------
width : float
    Width of the window.
height : float
    Height of the window.

Returns:
-------
Shapely Polygon object
    A polygon representing the observation window.

functions.name: pySpat.utils.from_dataframe
documentation:
Convert a DataFrame of coordinates into a list of Shapely Points.

Parameters:
----------
dataframe : pandas DataFrame
    A dataframe containing 'x' and 'y' columns representing coordinates of points.

Returns:
-------
list of Shapely Point objects
    A list of point objects.

functions.name: pySpat.utils.to_dataframe
documentation:
Convert a list of Shapely Points into a DataFrame.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    A list of point objects.

Returns:
-------
pandas DataFrame
    A dataframe containing 'x' and 'y' columns.

functions.name: pySpat.utils.distance_matrix
documentation:
Compute the distance matrix for a set of points.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The list of points.

Returns:
-------
numpy.ndarray
    A square matrix containing distances between points.

functions.name: pySpat.visualisation.plot_point_pattern
documentation:
Plot the spatial point pattern within a given observation window.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The points in the spatial pattern.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.
title : str, optional
    The title of the plot (default is "Spatial Point Pattern").

functions.name: pySpat.visualisation.plot_intensity
documentation:
Plot the intensity of the spatial point pattern.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The points in the spatial pattern.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.
intensity_func : function
    A function that computes intensity (e.g., point density or kernel density estimate).
title : str, optional
    The title of the plot (default is "Point Intensity").

functions.name: pySpat.visualisation.plot_clusters
documentation:
Plot the clustered points within a given observation window.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The points in the spatial pattern.
cluster_radius : float
    Radius to define clusters.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.
title : str, optional
    The title of the plot (default is "Clustered Points").

functions.name: pySpat.visualisation.plot_strata
documentation:
Plot the spatially stratified point pattern within a given observation window.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The points in the spatial pattern.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.
strata_width : float
    Width of each stratum.
strata_height : float
    Height of each stratum.
title : str, optional
    The title of the plot (default is "Stratified Points").

functions.name: pySpat.visualisation.plot_ripley_l
documentation:
Plot Ripley's L function for a spatial point pattern.

Parameters:
----------
spatial_pattern : SpatialPointPattern object
    The spatial point pattern to analyze.
max_distance : float
    The maximum distance to calculate the function.
step : float, optional
    The step size for distance calculation (default is 0.1).
title : str, optional
    The title of the plot (default is "Ripley's L function").

functions.name: pySpat.model_fitting.fit_poisson_process
documentation:
Fit a Poisson process to the spatial point pattern.

Parameters:
----------
spatial_pattern : SpatialPointPattern object
    The spatial point pattern to fit.

Returns:
-------
float
    The intensity (lambda) of the Poisson process.

functions.name: pySpat.model_fitting.fit_clustered_process
documentation:
Fit a clustered process to the spatial point pattern.

Parameters:
----------
spatial_pattern : SpatialPointPattern object
    The spatial point pattern to fit.
cluster_radius : float
    The radius around each point to consider as a cluster.

Returns:
-------
dict
    A dictionary with:
    - 'mean_cluster_size': Mean number of points per cluster.

functions.name: pySpat.model_fitting.fit_stratified_process
documentation:
Fit a spatially stratified process to the spatial point pattern.

Parameters:
----------
spatial_pattern : SpatialPointPattern object
    The spatial point pattern to fit.
strata_width : float
    Width of each stratum.
strata_height : float
    Height of each stratum.

Returns:
-------
dict
    A dictionary with:
    - 'strata_distribution': Number of points per stratum.

classes.name: pySpat.model_fitting.SpatialPointPattern

methods.name: pySpat.model_fitting.SpatialPointPattern.__init__
documentation:
Constructs all the necessary attributes for the spatial point pattern object.

Parameters:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The points in the spatial point pattern.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.

methods.name: pySpat.model_fitting.SpatialPointPattern.from_dataframe
documentation:
Initialize the spatial point pattern from a dataframe with 'x' and 'y' columns.

Parameters:
----------
dataframe : pandas DataFrame
    A dataframe containing 'x' and 'y' columns representing coordinates of points.

methods.name: pySpat.model_fitting.SpatialPointPattern.summary_statistics
documentation:
Calculate summary statistics for the spatial point pattern.

Returns:
-------
dict
    A dictionary containing summary statistics:
    - 'total_points': Number of points in the pattern.
    - 'mean_distance': Mean distance between points.
    - 'min_distance': Minimum distance between points.
    - 'max_distance': Maximum distance between points.

A class to represent a spatial point pattern.

Attributes:
----------
points : list of Shapely Point objects
    The points in the spatial point pattern.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.

functions.name: pySpat.hypothesis_testing.poisson_test
documentation:
Perform a Poisson test for spatial randomness.

Parameters:
----------
observed : int
    The observed number of points in the spatial pattern.
expected : float
    The expected number of points based on intensity.
alpha : float, optional
    Significance level for the test (default is 0.05).

Returns:
-------
bool
    True if the pattern is significantly random, False otherwise.

functions.name: pySpat.hypothesis_testing.chi_square_test
documentation:
Perform a Chi-Square test for spatial stratification.

Parameters:
----------
observed_counts : list of int
    Observed counts of points in different strata.
expected_counts : list of float
    Expected counts of points in different strata based on uniform distribution.
alpha : float, optional
    Significance level for the test (default is 0.05).

Returns:
-------
bool
    True if the pattern is significantly different from uniform, False otherwise.

functions.name: pySpat.summary_statistics.ripley_k
documentation:
Calculate Ripley's K function for a set of points.

```
param points

A list of Shapely Point objects.

param r

The distance at which to compute K(r).

return

Ripley's K(r) value.
```

functions.name: pySpat.summary_statistics.ripley_l
documentation:
Calculate Ripley's L function for a set of points.

```
param points

A list of Shapely Point objects.

param r

The distance at which to compute L(r).

return

Ripley's L(r) value.
```

functions.name: pySpat.summary_statistics.pair_correlation_function
documentation:
Calculate the pair correlation function g(r).

```
param points

A list of Shapely Point objects.

param r

The distance at which to compute g(r).

return

g(r) value.
```

functions.name: pySpat.summary_statistics.nearest_neighbor_distance
documentation:
Calculate the nearest neighbor distance for a set of points.

```
param points

A list of Shapely Point objects.

return

The mean nearest neighbor distance.
```

functions.name: pySpat.summary_statistics.density_estimate
documentation:
Estimate the density of points in a window.

```
param points

A list of Shapely Point objects.

param window

A Shapely Polygon object representing the observation window.

return

Estimated density of points per unit area.
```

functions.name: pySpat.summary_statistics.summary_statistics
documentation:
Calculate various summary statistics for a point pattern.

```
param points

A list of Shapely Point objects.

param window

A Shapely Polygon object representing the observation window.

return

A dictionary with statistics including intensity, clustering measure, etc.
```

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.PointPattern.__init__
documentation:
Represents a spatial point pattern.
\:param points: List of tuples [(x1, y1), (x2, y2), ...]
\:param window: A Shapely Polygon defining the observation area.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.PointPattern.n_points
documentation: Return the number of points in the pattern.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.PointPattern.intensity
documentation: Calculate the intensity (points per unit area).

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.ObservationWindow.__init__
documentation:
Represents an observation window in 2D space.
\:param boundary: A list of (x, y) tuples defining the boundary of the window,

> or a Shapely Polygon object.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.ObservationWindow.area
documentation: Returns the area of the observation window.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.ObservationWindow.perimeter
documentation: Returns the perimeter of the observation window.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.ObservationWindow.contains_point
documentation:
Checks if a given point is inside the observation window.
\:param point: A tuple (x, y) or a Shapely Point object.
\:return: True if the point is inside the window, False otherwise.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.ObservationWindow.intersects
documentation:
Checks if this observation window intersects with another window.
\:param other_window: An ObservationWindow object.
\:return: True if the two windows intersect, False otherwise.

methods.name: pySpat.point_pattern.ObservationWindow.intersection
documentation:
Computes the intersection of this window with another window.
\:param other_window: An ObservationWindow object.
\:return: A new ObservationWindow representing the intersection.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.random_window
documentation:
Generate a random observation window within given bounds.

Parameters:
----------
bounds : tuple
    A tuple of coordinates representing the bounding box (minx, miny, maxx, maxy).

Returns:
-------
Shapely Polygon object
    A random observation window.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.calculate_distance
documentation:
Calculate the Euclidean distance between two points.

Parameters:
----------
point1 : Shapely Point object
    First point.
point2 : Shapely Point object
    Second point.

Returns:
-------
float
    Distance between the two points.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.check_within_window
documentation:
Check if a point is within the given observation window.

Parameters:
----------
point : Shapely Point object
    The point to check.
window : Shapely Polygon object
    The observation window.

Returns:
-------
bool
    True if the point is within the window, False otherwise.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.check_overlap
documentation:
Check if two observation windows overlap.

Parameters:
----------
window1 : Shapely Polygon object
    First observation window.
window2 : Shapely Polygon object
    Second observation window.

Returns:
-------
bool
    True if the windows overlap, False otherwise.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.calculate_area
documentation:
Calculate the area of a polygon.

Parameters:
----------
polygon : Shapely Polygon object
    The polygon for which to calculate the area.

Returns:
-------
float
    Area of the polygon.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.calculate_centroid
documentation:
Calculate the centroid of a polygon.

Parameters:
----------
polygon : Shapely Polygon object
    The polygon for which to calculate the centroid.

Returns:
-------
Shapely Point object
    The centroid of the polygon.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.get_bounding_box
documentation:
Get the bounding box of a polygon.

Parameters:
----------
polygon : Shapely Polygon object
    The polygon for which to get the bounding box.

Returns:
-------
tuple
    A tuple of coordinates representing the bounding box (minx, miny, maxx, maxy).

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.nearest_neighbor
documentation:
Find the nearest neighbor point from a list of points.

Parameters:
----------
point : Shapely Point object
    The reference point.
points_list : list of Shapely Point objects
    List of points to compare against.

Returns:
-------
Shapely Point object
    The nearest neighbor point.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.point_in_polygon
documentation:
Check if a point is inside a given polygon.

Parameters:
----------
point : Shapely Point object
    The point to check.
polygon : Shapely Polygon object
    The polygon in which to check.

Returns:
-------
bool
    True if the point is inside the polygon, False otherwise.

functions.name: pySpat.geometry.calculate_distance_to_boundary
documentation:
Calculate the distance from a point to the nearest boundary of a polygon.

Parameters:
----------
point : Shapely Point object
    The point.
polygon : Shapely Polygon object
    The polygon.

Returns:
-------
float
    The distance to the nearest boundary.

functions.name: pySpat.simulation.generate_poisson_points
documentation:
Generate a random point pattern using a Poisson process.

```
param window

A Shapely Polygon object representing the observation window.

param intensity

Intensity of the Poisson process (number of points per unit area).

param n_points

Optional number of points to generate. If not provided, the total number of points is
determined based on the intensity and the area of the window.

return

A list of Shapely Point objects.
```

functions.name: pySpat.simulation.generate_cluster_points
documentation:
Generate a clustered point pattern.

```
param window

A Shapely Polygon object representing the observation window.

param n_clusters

Number of clusters to generate.

param points_per_cluster

Number of points per cluster.

param cluster_radius

Radius within which points are clustered.

return

A list of Shapely Point objects.
```

functions.name: pySpat.simulation.generate_stratified_points
documentation:
Generate a spatially stratified random point pattern.

```
param window

A Shapely Polygon object representing the observation window.

param n_strata

Number of strata to divide the window into.

param points_per_stratum

Number of points per stratum.

return

A list of Shapely Point objects.
```

functions.name: pySpat.simulation.simulate_random_patterns
documentation:
Simulate multiple random spatial point patterns.

```
param window

A Shapely Polygon object representing the observation window.

param n_patterns

Number of patterns to generate.

param intensity

Intensity for Poisson point pattern.

param n_clusters

Number of clusters for clustered pattern.

param points_per_cluster

Points per cluster for clustered pattern.

param cluster_radius

Radius of clusters.

param points_per_stratum

Points per stratum for stratified pattern.

param n_strata

Number of strata for stratified pattern.

return

A list of lists containing Shapely Point objects for each pattern.
```
